text.title: JPA/Hibernate Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules grounding production JPA + Hibernate on Spring Boot.

rule.statement: Every entity MUST carry created/updated timestamps via `@CreationTimestamp` + `@UpdateTimestamp` (Hibernate) or `@CreatedDate` + `@LastModifiedDate` (Spring Data JPA with `@EntityListeners(AuditingEntityListener.class)`). `createdAt` MUST be `updatable=false`.
rule.rationale: Auditing fields support compliance + debugging at near-zero cost; missing them forces post-hoc trigger work later.

rule.statement: Every `@OneToMany`/`@ManyToMany` MUST have an explicit Javadoc comment justifying the cascade + orphanRemoval + fetch choice. `CascadeType.ALL` is forbidden without a written reason; default `FetchType.LAZY` is preserved; `EAGER` requires justification.
rule.rationale: Unjustified `CascadeType.ALL` deletes data; `EAGER` collections cause Cartesian explosions.

rule.statement: Services MUST consume a narrow repository interface listing only the operations actually used. Raw `JpaRepository<T, ID>` MUST NOT appear in service method signatures. `JpaSpecificationExecutor` is added only when dynamic filtering is required.
rule.rationale: Exposing the full CRUD surface tempts callers to bypass the aggregate boundary; narrow interfaces are testable + auditable.

rule.statement: Read-only queries that don't need the full entity MUST project to a DTO (interface or class projection). Loading a full `@Entity` to populate a 3-field list view is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Entity hydration costs hundreds of bytes per row even when the caller wants three columns; projection short-circuits the cost.

rule.statement: Eager loading of an association inside the transactional boundary MUST use `JOIN FETCH` in `@Query` or `@EntityGraph` on the repository method. Lazy associations MUST NOT be accessed outside `@Transactional` (no controller-/view-side lazy loading; `spring.jpa.open-in-view=false`).
rule.rationale: Lazy access outside session throws `LazyInitializationException`; open-in-view hides the problem and inflates request times.
